steps.title: Use the isosceles condition
steps.content: Since $RT = TU$, triangle $RTU$ is isosceles, so its base angles are equal: $\angle TRU = \angle TUR$. The exterior angle at $T$ (marked $114°$) is supplementary to the interior angle $\angle RTU$, so $\angle RTU = 180° - 114° = 66°$. Therefore each base angle is $\frac{180° - 66°}{2} = 57°$.

steps.title: Find the angles at R from triangle STR
steps.content: The exterior angle of triangle $STR$ at vertex $T$ equals the sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles: $114° = \angle SRT + \angle RST$. From the figure, $\angle RST = 50°$, so $\angle SRT = 114° - 50° = 64°$.

steps.title: Identify x
steps.content: From the figure, $x = \angle SRT = 64°$. The answer is 64 — that matches choice C.
